text: We first focus on integration of functions on finite intervals in ℝ . We can integrate elementary functions.
html: We first focus on integration of functions on finite intervals in ℝ. We can integrate elementary functions.
zh: 首先关注 ℝ 中有限区间上函数的积分。可以对初等函数进行积分。

text: The fundamental theorem of calculus relates integration and differentiation. Below we give simplified statements of the two parts of this theorem. The first part says that integration provides an inverse to differentiation and the second one specifies how to compute integrals of derivatives. (These two parts are very closely related, but their optimal versions, which are not shown here, are not equivalent.)
html: The fundamental theorem of calculus relates integration and differentiation. Below we give simplified statements of the two parts of this theorem. The first part says that integration provides an inverse to differentiation and the second one specifies how to compute integrals of derivatives. (These two parts are very closely related, but their optimal versions, which are not shown here, are not equivalent.)
zh: 微积分基本定理把积分与微分联系起来。下面给出该定理两部分的简化陈述。第一部分说，积分提供了微分的逆运算；第二部分说明如何计算导数的积分。（这两部分非常密切相关，但这里没有展示的最佳版本并不等价。）

text: Convolution is also defined in Mathlib and its basic properties are proved.
html: Convolution is also defined in Mathlib and its basic properties are proved.
zh: 卷积也在 Mathlib 中定义，并且其基本性质已经得到证明。

text: The general context for integration in Mathlib is measure theory. Even the elementary integrals of the previous section are in fact Bochner integrals. Bochner integration is a generalization of Lebesgue integration where the target space can be any Banach space, not necessarily finite dimensional.
html: The general context for integration in Mathlib is measure theory. Even the elementary integrals of the previous section are in fact Bochner integrals. Bochner integration is a generalization of Lebesgue integration where the target space can be any Banach space, not necessarily finite dimensional.
zh: Mathlib 中积分的一般语境是测度论。甚至上一节中的初等积分，实际上也是 Bochner 积分。Bochner 积分是 Lebesgue 积分的一种推广，其中目标空间可以是任意 Banach 空间，而不必有限维。

text: The first component in the development of measure theory is the notion of a \(\sigma\) -algebra of sets, which are called the measurable sets. The type class MeasurableSpace serves to equip a type with such a structure. The sets empty and univ are measurable, the complement of a measurable set is measurable, and a countable union or intersection of measurable sets is measurable. Note that these axioms are redundant; if you #print MeasurableSpace , you will see the ones that Mathlib uses. As the examples below show, countability assumptions can be expressed using the Encodable type class.
html: The first component in the development of measure theory is the notion of a \(\sigma\)-algebra of sets, which are called the measurable sets. The type class MeasurableSpace serves to equip a type with such a structure. The sets empty and univ are measurable, the complement of a measurable set is measurable, and a countable union or intersection of measurable sets is measurable. Note that these axioms are redundant; if you #print MeasurableSpace, you will see the ones that Mathlib uses. As the examples below show, countability assumptions can be expressed using the Encodable type class.
zh: 测度论发展的第一个组成部分，是集合的 \(\sigma\)-代数概念；其中的集合称为可测集合。类型类 MeasurableSpace 用来给一个类型配备这种结构。集合 empty 与 univ 是可测的；可测集的补集是可测的；可测集的可数并或可数交也是可测的。注意，这些公理是冗余的；如果执行 #print MeasurableSpace，就会看到 Mathlib 实际采用的公理。如下例所示，可数性假设可以用类型类 Encodable 表达。

text: Once a type is measurable, we can measure it. On paper, a measure on a set (or type) equipped with a \(\sigma\) -algebra is a function from the measurable sets to the extended non-negative reals that is additive on countable disjoint unions. In Mathlib, we don’t want to carry around measurability assumptions every time we write an application of the measure to a set. So we extend the measure to any set s as the infimum of measures of measurable sets containing s . Of course, many lemmas still require measurability assumptions, but not all.
html: Once a type is measurable, we can measure it. On paper, a measure on a set (or type) equipped with a \(\sigma\)-algebra is a function from the measurable sets to the extended non-negative reals that is additive on countable disjoint unions. In Mathlib, we don’t want to carry around measurability assumptions every time we write an application of the measure to a set. So we extend the measure to any set s as the infimum of measures of measurable sets containing s. Of course, many lemmas still require measurability assumptions, but not all.
zh: 一旦一个类型是可测的，就可以对它作测度。纸笔数学中，在配备 \(\sigma\)-代数的集合（或类型）上，一个测度是从可测集到扩展非负实数的函数，并且对可数不交并满足可加性。在 Mathlib 中，我们不希望每次把测度应用于集合时都携带可测性假设。因此，我们把测度扩展到任意集合 s，定义为包含 s 的可测集的测度的下确界。当然，许多引理仍需要可测性假设，但并非全部如此。

text: Once a type has a measure associated with it, we say that a property P holds almost everywhere if the set of elements where the property fails has measure 0. The collection of properties that hold almost everywhere form a filter, but Mathlib introduces special notation for saying that a property holds almost everywhere.
html: Once a type has a measure associated with it, we say that a property P holds almost everywhere if the set of elements where the property fails has measure 0. The collection of properties that hold almost everywhere form a filter, but Mathlib introduces special notation for saying that a property holds almost everywhere.
zh: 一旦某个类型关联了测度，如果性质 P 不成立的元素组成的集合测度为 0，就说 P几乎处处成立。几乎处处成立的性质组成一个滤子，但 Mathlib 引入了专门记号来表达性质几乎处处成立。

text: Now that we have measurable spaces and measures we can consider integrals. As explained above, Mathlib uses a very general notion of integration that allows any Banach space as the target. As usual, we don’t want our notation to carry around assumptions, so we define integration in such a way that an integral is equal to zero if the function in question is not integrable. Most lemmas having to do with integrals have integrability assumptions.
html: Now that we have measurable spaces and measures we can consider integrals. As explained above, Mathlib uses a very general notion of integration that allows any Banach space as the target. As usual, we don’t want our notation to carry around assumptions, so we define integration in such a way that an integral is equal to zero if the function in question is not integrable. Most lemmas having to do with integrals have integrability assumptions.
zh: 现在有了可测空间和测度，就可以考虑积分。如上所述，Mathlib 使用非常一般的积分概念，允许任何 Banach 空间作为目标。照常，我们不希望记号携带各种假设，因此把积分定义成：若所讨论的函数不可积，则其积分等于零。多数与积分有关的引理都带有可积性假设。

text: As an example of the complex interactions between our various conventions, let us see how to integrate constant functions. Recall that a measure μ takes values in ℝ≥0∞ , the type of extended non-negative reals. There is a function ENNReal.toReal : ℝ≥0∞ → ℝ which sends ⊤ , the point at infinity, to zero. For any s : Set α , if μ s = ⊤ , then nonzero constant functions are not integrable on s . In that case, their integrals are equal to zero by definition, as is (μ s).toReal . So in all cases we have the following lemma.
html: As an example of the complex interactions between our various conventions, let us see how to integrate constant functions. Recall that a measure μ takes values in ℝ≥0∞, the type of extended non-negative reals. There is a function ENNReal.toReal : ℝ≥0∞ → ℝ which sends ⊤, the point at infinity, to zero. For any s : Set α, if μ s = ⊤, then nonzero constant functions are not integrable on s. In that case, their integrals are equal to zero by definition, as is (μ s).toReal. So in all cases we have the following lemma.
zh: 作为各种约定之间复杂相互作用的例子，来看如何积分常值函数。回忆测度 μ 取值于 ℝ≥0∞，即扩展非负实数类型。有一个函数 ENNReal.toReal : ℝ≥0∞ → ℝ，它把无穷远点 ⊤ 送到零。对任意 s : Set α，若 μ s = ⊤，则非零常值函数在 s 上不可积。在这种情况下，它们的积分按定义等于零，而 (μ s).toReal 也是如此。因此，在所有情形中都有下面的引理。

text: We now quickly explain how to access the most important theorems in integration theory, starting with the dominated convergence theorem. There are several versions in Mathlib, and here we only show the most basic one.
html: We now quickly explain how to access the most important theorems in integration theory, starting with the dominated convergence theorem. There are several versions in Mathlib, and here we only show the most basic one.
zh: 现在快速说明如何访问积分理论中最重要的定理，从支配收敛定理开始。Mathlib 中有若干版本，这里只展示最基本的一个。

text: Then we have Fubini’s theorem for integrals on product type.
html: Then we have Fubini’s theorem for integrals on product type.
zh: 随后有关于积类型上积分的 Fubini 定理。

text: There is a very general version of convolution that applies to any continuous bilinear form.
html: There is a very general version of convolution that applies to any continuous bilinear form.
zh: 卷积还有一个非常一般的版本，适用于任意连续双线性型。

text: Finally, Mathlib has a very general version of the change-of-variables formula. In the statement below, BorelSpace E means the \(\sigma\) -algebra on E is generated by the open sets of E , and IsAddHaarMeasure μ means that the measure μ is left-invariant, gives finite mass to compact sets, and give positive mass to open sets.
html: Finally, Mathlib has a very general version of the change-of-variables formula. In the statement below, BorelSpace E means the \(\sigma\)-algebra on E is generated by the open sets of E, and IsAddHaarMeasure μ means that the measure μ is left-invariant, gives finite mass to compact sets, and give positive mass to open sets.
zh: 最后，Mathlib 有一个非常一般的变量代换公式版本。在下面的陈述中，BorelSpace E 表示 E 上的 \(\sigma\)-代数由 E 的开集生成；IsAddHaarMeasure μ 表示测度 μ 左不变，对紧集赋予有限质量，并对开集赋予正质量。
